title: Indirect immunofluorescence with preextraction (in situ cell fractionation)
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"uio1n","text":"Immunostaining of DDR proteins in senescent cells\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":49}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gkf7y","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"tcoi1","text":"The study of the activation of DNA damage response factors at the single cell level relies on the observation of their accumulation (or the accumulation of their activated forms) in nuclear foci by plain immunofluorescence techniques. Nevertheless, a number of them such as ATR, ATRIP and RPA do not form detectable foci if their excess is not first removed by a brief treatment that washes away the fraction that is not chromatin bound. This describes here this technique first reported by Mirzoeva, O. K. \u0026 Petrini, J. H. J.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

G-7765) in PBS 1x\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cp3nj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"l8hic","text":"DAPI ( SIGMA cat.D-9564)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ranzd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qecqh","text":"Mowiol mounting medium ( CALBIOCHEM  cat. 475904)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d4ahd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eynky","text":"Primary Antibodies: RPA /p34 (Neomarkers Cat. #MS-691-P1); 1:300\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ulnqn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"0kdko","text":"ATR goat (Santa Cruz Biotechnologies N-19) 1:50","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare poly-D-lysinated coverslips (poly D lysine 50/μg/ml)
Plate 15-20x10 3 cells on coverslips 1 day before the staining
Wash cells grown on coverslips gently 2x with ice-cold PBS 1x
Treat the cells with ice-cold cytoskeleton buffer 5’ on ice
Aspirate and add an ice-cold cytoskeleton stripping buffer for 5’ on ice
Wash 3x with ice-cold PBS
Fix the cells in fixative for 30’ RT
Wash in 2x PBS 1x
Permeabilize for 15’ at RT with permeabilization buffer
Wash 3x in PBS 1x
Block cells for 30’ with PBG 1X
Stain with primary antibody diluted in PBG 1X at indicated concentration for 1h at RT
Wash cells gently 3X5’ in PBG 1X
Stain with secondary antibody diluted in PBG 1X for 1h at RT
Wash cells gently 2X5’ in PBG 1x and 2X5’ in PBS 1X
Stain with Dapi for 5' in the dark
Mount the coverslips with 3 μl of Mowiol mounting medium
Let the coverslips dry before microscope analysis
Higher possibility to lose cells because of different steps. Always use poli D-lysinated coverslips especially for RAS-induced senescent cells
Oncogene-induced senescence is a DNA damage response triggered by DNA hyper-replication by Di Micco, R. et al. published in Nature on 03 Oct, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature05327
Figure 1. Confocal analysis of oncogene-induced senescent cells immunostained for RPA/ATR/ATRIP complex by indirect immunofluorescence with preextraction
